name: engineering-leadership
description: Codex skills for engineering team leads: one-on-ones, feedback and reviews, project breakdown, team health, hard conversations, hiring, and career growth. Derived from Camille Fournier's The Manager's Path.

interface.shortDescription: Team-lead skills for 1-1s, feedback, planning, hiring, and growth.
interface.longDescription: A Codex plugin that bundles reusable skills for the daily work of an engineering team lead: running 1-1s that surface real signal, giving feedback and writing reviews with no surprises, breaking down multi-week projects, debugging team health, having hard conversations, designing hiring loops, and guiding career growth. Skills are grounded in Camille Fournier's The Manager's Path.
defaultPrompt: Use engineering-leadership skills to help me lead my team this week.
Use one-on-ones to prep my 1-1 with this report.
Use one-on-ones to extract follow-ups from these 1-1 notes.
Use feedback-and-reviews to draft this performance review without surprises.
Use feedback-and-reviews to plan how to give this piece of feedback.
Use project-breakdown to break down this multi-week project into milestones.
Use project-breakdown to decide what to cut as the deadline approaches.
Use team-health-debugger to diagnose why my team feels off.
Use hard-conversations to prepare for telling this engineer they are not getting promoted.
Use hard-conversations to plan how to say no to this leadership ask.
Use hiring-and-interviewing to design an interview loop for this role.
Use hiring-and-interviewing to debrief and calibrate after this onsite.
Use career-growth to build a growth plan for this engineer.
Use career-growth to evaluate whether this engineer is ready for the next level.
